name: Madison Square Garden Sports Corp.
business: Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. owns and operates professional sports franchises, primarily the New York Knicks (NBA) and New York Rangers (NHL), as well as development teams. Revenue is generated from ticket sales, media rights (including local media rights fees from MSG Networks), sponsorships, suite rentals, food and beverage sales, and merchandise.

exposures.sentence: The Company owns and operates a portfolio of assets featuring some of the most recognized teams in all of sports, including the New York Knickerbockers (Knicks) of the National Basketball Association (NBA) and the New York Rangers (Rangers) of the National Hockey League (NHL).

We are Subject to Data Privacy, Data Protection and Data Security Regulations and Laws and Could Face Substantial Penalties if We Fail to Comply With Such Regulations and Laws and We Are Subject to Governmental Regulation, Which Can Change, and Any Failure to Comply With These Regulations May Have a Material Negative Effect on Our Business and Results of Operations.

catalysts.event: NBA entered into new 11-year media rights agreements taking effect starting with 2025-26 season
catalysts.sentence: In July 2024, the NBA entered into new 11-year media rights agreements with The Walt Disney Company, NBCUniversal and Amazon, which will take effect starting with 2025-26 season and will expire following the 2035-36 season.

catalysts.event: NHL and Rogers Communications entered into new 12-year media rights agreement beginning with 2026-27 season
catalysts.sentence: in April 2025, the NHL and Rogers Communications entered into a new 12-year media rights agreement beginning with the 2026-27 season.

catalysts.event: Local media rights agreements amended with fee reductions effective January 1, 2025, and expiration end of 2028-29 season
catalysts.sentence: On June 27, 2025, the media rights agreements between subsidiaries of MSG Networks, on the one hand, and Knicks LLC and Rangers LLC, on the other hand, were amended, as follows: New York Knicks: A modification to the annual rights fee to effect a 28% reduction as of January 1, 2025; an elimination of the annual rights fee escalator; and a change to the contract expiration date to the end of the 2028-29 season, subject to a right of first refusal in favor of MSG Networks; New York Rangers: A modification to the annual rights fee to effect an 18% reduction as of January 1, 2025; an elimination of the annual rights fee escalator; and a change to the contract expiration date to the end of the 2028-29 season, subject to a right of first refusal in favor of MSG Networks;

catalysts.event: New NHL CBA ratified effective for 2025-26 season through 2029-30
catalysts.sentence: On July 8, 2025, the NHL and the National Hockey League Players Association (NHLPA) announced that a new four-year CBA had been ratified by the NHL Board of Governors and the NHL players. The new NHL CBA expires after the 2029-30 season.

catalysts.event: Knicks Revolving Credit Facility expires in December 2026
catalysts.sentence: Both credit facilities expire in December 2026.
